segments: Deborah : Hey Jolene, nice to meet you! How ' s your week going? Anything fun happened? Jolene : Hi Deb! Good to meet you! Yeah my week ' s been busy. finished an electrical engineering project last week - took lot of work it ' s done now. Anything fun happening for you? Deborah : Congrats! Last week visited a place holds memories for me my mother s old house. Jolene : Why does it hold such special memories for you? Deborah : full of memories, she passed away few years ago. our last photo together. Jolene : Sorry about your loss, Deb. My mother also passed away last year. my room in her house have many memories there. anything special about it you remember? Deborah : My mom ' s house had special bench near the window. She loved to sit there every morning take in view. come to sit here sometimes helps me stay connected to her. Jolene : Staying connected important. something to remember her by? pendant reminds me of my mother gave it to me in 2010 in Paris. Deborah : Yes, also have a pendant reminds me of my mother. what special for you about your jewelry? Jolene : has special symbol represents freedom for me nice reminder to go for my goals not get held back. Deborah : should give you strength and energy! Jolene : have goals? Deborah : goals is to keep teaching yoga supporting my community. passionate about helping people find peace and joy through. Jolene : inspired you to go down this route? Deborah : Yoga helped me find peace during rough time now passionate about sharing that with others. Jolene : truly inspiring! Deborah : Gotta run, bye! Jolene : Looking forward to next chat

Deborah : Hey Jolene, sorry to tell you this but my dad passed away two days ago. ' s been tough on us all - his sudden death left us all kinda shell - shocked. trying to channel my grief by spending more time with family cherishing memories. moments remind me to live life fully. Jolene : Sorry to hear about your dad, Deborah. Losing parent is tough - how ' s it going for you and your family? Deborah : Even s hard comforting to look back on great memories. looked at the family album. Photos give me peace during difficult times. my parents ' wedding in 1993. Jolene : They beautiful couple! Deborah : My husband and I trying to be as good a family as my parents were! Jolene : What do you value in your relationship? Deborah : love, openness kept us close all these years. Being there for each other made us both happy. Look what letter received yesterday! Jolene : touching words! Who is this letter from? Deborah : group members sent this to me! thanked me for positive influence on. moments remind me why passionate about yoga. Jolene : Where do you most often do yoga? Deborah : one of places where I do it. Jolene : Where is it? Deborah : my old home. go there now then for my mom, who passed away. Sitting in spot by window gives me peace. Jolene : Must great to have place where feel connected to her. Deborah : s special. can feel her presence comforts me. Jolene : sounds spot holds sentimental value. bring back any special memories? Deborah : Yeah, Jolene. ' d sit there every night with book and smile, reading was one of her hobbies one of her favorite places in house. Jolene : What other hobbies did your mother have? Deborah : Travel was her great passion! Jolene : want to show you one of my snakes! They calm me down make me happy. This is SusieDeborah : Having a pet brightens up your life. ' s great it brings you comfort. any fun moments with your pet like to share? Jolene : playing video games my pet slinked out of her cage coiled up next to me - too funny! second snake Seraphim did it. Look at her sly eyes! Deborah : Awww so nice! Jolene : bought it a year ago in Paris. Deborah : Cool, Jolene! Pets bring much happiness! Jolene : very unusual pets! Here ' s me and my partner gaming last week - fun. played game " Detroit " on console. both crazy about this activity! Deborah : your boyfriend teach you to play? Jolene : Even as child learned to play on my own. Deborah : only play old games or try new ones? Jolene : planning to play " Walking Dead " next Saturday. Deborah : Take care and keep spreading good vibes! Jolene : Thanks, Deb! You too, take care. See ya

Jolene : Hi Deb! How ' re you? I ' ve been busy. My engineering professor gave us a huge robotics project. It ' s tough but fun, making me get creative and problem - solve. Deborah : Hey Jolene! s great to hear from you. sounds challenging but interesting! must be putting your creative and problem - solving skills to test. How did you feel when you first received project? enjoying working on it? Jolene : When got it felt mix of emotions - excited and nervous. But now enjoying it! s like trying to solve a puzzle, figuring out best design and programming. Seeing robot come together is awesome too! Deborah : That ' s awesome, Jolene! enjoying process. must be satisfying to see it come together. Keep up good work! by met new neighbor Anna yesterday! Jolene : How did you two meet? Deborah : happened at yoga in the park. Jolene : Wow awesome! great connecting with people similar interests. Did you two talk about it? Deborah : Yeah talked about how it improved our lives sense of community it gives. Jolene : Sounds great! Deborah : thought about resuming yoga? Jolene :.. planned to play the console with my partner. Deborah : s also good that you have something to do together. Jolene : Thanks for kind words! Deborah : Gotta run bye! Jolene : See you soon

Jolene : Hey Deborah! Good to hear from you. How ' ve you been? I ' been on an emotional rollercoaster lately, I ' m coping. Deborah : Hey Jolene! Good to hear from you. All good here - how about you? Anything new happening lately? Jolene : had major milestone last week it went well - relieved and proud. huge accomplishment for me as an engineer. Deborah : That ' s awesome. You must have worked hard for that. proud of you. Care to share more about it? Jolene : Thanks so much! had to plan and research a lot to design build a sustainable water purifier for rural community in need. tough, but loved the experience. Deborah : Your engineering skills made difference for people in rural area. That ' s amazing! How did it feel when saw it working? Jolene : surreal moment. Seeing working providing clean water to community was satisfying. reminded me of how engineering can make difference in people ' s lives. made me feel like I had a purpose had done something good. Deborah : what are your career aspirations now? Jolene : want to keep working in engineering continue make positive impact on communities in need by creating sustainable solutions. goal is to contribute towards making world better place with my work. Deborah : sure great things will keep coming your way. Keep up good work follow your passions! Jolene : Your support means a lot. determined to keep going make a difference. Your encouragement motivates me to pursue my passions. Deborah : When things get tough take deep breath remember why you ' re doing this. spend a of time, teaching yoga. great way to find balance reconnect with ourselves. bonded with Anna during yesterday ' s l yoga class. Jolene : Wow awesome! What new poses you try? Deborah : trying out different dance poses, like Warrior II, builds strength boosts focus.Jolene : difficult to do? Deborah : Yes, this brought us closer to Anna! supported each other, means a lot. Jolene : explain how to do it? Deborah : Sure! To do modified pose, sit on edge of a chair with your feet planted. Twist your torso to one side use hand on knee for support. ll feel a stretch in your back and shoulders as hold it for few breaths, then switch sides. great for relaxing tense muscles. Jolene : Sounds really hard! Here ' s my bookshelf! Deborah : s quite a collection! had favorite book lately? love to hear your thoughts. Jolene : Thanks Deborah! m into this book called " Sapiens " - fascinating look at human history and technology affected us. giving me a lot to think about! Deborah : Great, interesting! come across any recent ones that struck you? Jolene : Two weeks ago read " Avalanche " by Neal Stephenson in one sitting! Deborah : sounds cool, Jolene. Stories can be powerful - can teach us, motivate us, bring us together. Jolene : also read recent story about someone who became successful despite facing numerous challenges. showed me anything is possible if you believe and work hard. Deborah : powerful message. reminds me of someone special and their grace and strength when faced hardships. Check out this creation! Jolene : Wow, Deb! ' s beautiful! tell backstory of that bouquet? Deborah : My friend gave me this bouquet when was struggling, gives me hope and courage. filled with warmth and appreciation when look at it. Jolene : ' s really sweet. small thing can make a real difference. Deborah : amazing how something as simple as flowers can make a real difference. Nature sure is beautiful. Jolene : Where are some of your favorite nature spots? Deborah : so many great places! favorite is a park with forest trail - so calming getting lost in nature.Then there ' s this beach nearby I love - sound of waves and sand under my feet make me feel all peaceful happy. any favorite spots, Jolene? Jolene : Here ' s a picture I took on vacation last summer in Bogota. beautiful calming watching sunset over water. made me appreciate nature ' s calming power. Deborah : sounds great, Jolene. Nature ' s calming for sure. it helps us forget daily craziness find inner peace. No wonder you ' re a fan! I like to come to this spot by water near my mom ' s old house. I reflect on her life find peace. Being surrounded by nature helps a lot. Jolene : ' s so touching! any little traditions when to reflect on your mom? Deborah : remember this amulet from her? Whenever come here I bring it with me. s how I feel her love stay close to her. Holding it brings me comfort. Jolene : s amazing how something small can have big impact,? Deborah : even small things like this can make big difference. reminder of all love and strength we have inside, connecting us to people we ' ve lost comforting us. Jolene : can keep them close in our hearts every day. Deborah : Anna has a pendant she wears in memory of her mother! brought us closer. Jolene : s nice to have reminders as source of strength when we need it. Deborah : Life ' s tough but hang in there. Look to your sources of strength you ' ll do great. Stay in touch, take care of yourself, know always here to cheer you on! Jolene : Thanks, Deb! Your encouragement means a lot to me. I ll stay in touch. Bye, take care and keep shining! Deborah : Stay safe! Bye

Jolene : Hey Deborah! Been few days since we last talked wanted to fill you in on something cool. Last Wednesday did a mini retreat to assess where I ' m at in life. dope experience gave me new outlook. Deborah : Hey Jolene! Sounds great. Taking time to reflect can be awesome. gain any new insights from? Jolene : achieved much more than imagined. real confidence boost. Deborah : deserve credit for stepping outside your comfort zone and believing in yourself. What cool stuff did you accomplish at retreat? Jolene : accomplished something with my engineering project - came up with neat solutions excited about it. Deborah : Let s go into more detail. Jolene : Green tech could make difference in disadvantaged areas. like to look into it see how I can contribute. speaking of helping out had idea : volunteer program where engineers teach STEM to underprivileged kids. What do think of that? Deborah : sounds great, Jolene! great way to help inspire others. They benefit a lot from your knowledge. Have thought of a plan yet? Jolene : Haven ' t finished planning yet but thinking of teaming up with local schools/centers to do workshops. could invite engineers as guest speakers to show kids career options. Deborah : Having guest speakers like them give kids real - world view. reached out to any schools or centers yet? Jolene : No, not yet. want to solidify the plan first. Can ' t wait to start reaching out,! Deborah : That makes sense. excited to hear how you reach out help those kids. Let me know how it goes! Jolene : I ' ll keep you posted! Appreciate the support! my sketches in planner. Deborah : Sounds like you ' re doing great. if need more tips or information. Jolene : Thanks, Deb! If need anything else ' ll let you know. You ' re awesome! Deborah : You ' re awesome too! Take care! Jolene : Stay safe

Jolene : Hey Deborah, totally buzzing! Had great night out last night - dinner, drinks with friends. glad I got to let my hair down. You? Deborah : Sounds great, Jolene! visited this place was so calming. Nostalgic too. Jolene : Wow flowers are beautiful! What type are they? looks peaceful there. Deborah : roses and dahlias bring me peace. lost a friend last week been spending time in garden to find comfort. Jolene : Sorry to hear about your friend, Deb. Losing someone can be tough. How are you holding up? Deborah : Thanks for kind words. s been tough, but comforted by remembering our time together reminds me of special life. Jolene : Memories can give us much comfort and joy. Deborah : Memories keep loved ones close. last photo with Karlie taken last summer when we hiked. was our last one. had such great time! Every time I see it can ' t help but smile. Jolene : Wow looks like great trip! Where else have you traveled? Deborah : been blessed to travel to few places Bali last year was one of my favs. gorgeous island gave me peace, great for yoga. Jolene : Wow ' s great! Is yoga on the beach a thing? been wanting to try it. Deborah : sound of waves fresh air wonderful! Jolene : ' ll give it a go! sounds peaceful. Thanks! Deborah : Let me know how it goes. Enjoy it! Jolene : ll keep you posted if I decide to go there. Deborah : Take care

Jolene : Hi Deborah, it ' s been a while! Since we last talked, so much has happened. Balancing engineering school with partner ' s video games is quite a feat. also setting aside time for myself, doing yoga and meditation. helps give me calm amidst craziness. Deborah : Hey Jolene! Great to hear from you. Taking a break is key. How have practices helping with everything? Jolene : They seriously saved me. I chill out gain perspective when I do yoga. Afterward more alert and motivated. my last photo. Deborah : Wow cool that yoga helping you out! Do they also do yoga with you? Does your partner do yoga with you? Jolene : He started joining me sometimes, nice for bonding and relaxing. brought us closer. just our random photo. Deborah : Aw s wonderful! How long you been married? Jolene : We re not married yet but been together for three years. taking it slow loving the ride. ring on his hand is just a decoration, not a wedding ring. Deborah : Sounds nice, Jolene. Enjoying journey main thing. how did you two meet? Jolene : met in engineering class in college quickly became friends. eventually blossomed into something more s been great! Deborah : your relationship started from strong friendship. still enjoy working on engineering project s together? Jolene : teaming up for these projects feels great! get each other ' s struggles both love it. Deborah : Have yoga or meditation helped with any stress? Jolene : Whenever overwhelmed take a break do some breathing or meditate. helps me feel calmer clearer to handle challenges more easily. Deborah : s been great to see your progress since we last chatted - keep it up! Jolene : Thanks, Deb! Your support means a lot. keep pushing forward continue taking time for myself. Deborah : Always rooting for youRemember to listen to your heart take good care. Here ' s a photo I took while reflecting outdoors. reminder to find peace even in chaos. Jolene : curious, what does your daily routine look like? Deborah : In morning meditate, do yoga teach classes. yesterday went for morning jog for first time in nearby park will now incorporate this into daily routine. in evenings spend time with loved ones. Jolene : Why you decide that? Deborah : Exercise is key for me - makes me feel connected to my body. Jolene : great healthy habit! Deborah : ' s like they say - " Can ' t pour from empty cup. " Looking out for ourselves gives us energy to help others. don ' t forget, you ' re worth it too! Jolene : Thanks, Deb. Your words mean something to me. keep remembering to prioritize self - care

Deborah : Hey Jolene, Anna got me a vegan stir - fry other day - tofu veg with ginger soy sauce. It was really tasty! Food wonderful source of pleasure nourishment. What dishes comforting to you? Jolene : One of my favorite dishes is lasagna! Comfort food can be great pick - me - up. got a lot going on with my studies and exams. Deborah : to find time for yourself lately? Jolene : trying to squeeze in me - time. Last Friday, did yoga and meditation to relax. find time for yourself too? Deborah : also did same, helped me reset my mind. How does it make you feel? Jolene : amazing few quiet moments can work wonders for soul. Deborah : to get outside lately? Jolene : take Seraphim to park last Sunday. She loved it here ' s a pic. Deborah : Looks like you had fun! Jolene : explored new places. People surprised when see tamed snake. What like about being outdoors? Deborah :.. birds chirping breeze gently blowing! reminds me of what really matters. Jolene : like reminder to slow down appreciate little things. Deborah : anything want to be more mindful of now? Jolene : need to be more mindful of stress levels take care of mental health. Sometimes get too caught up in studies forget to prioritize self - care. Deborah : Life can get hectic easy to forget about ourselves. Jolene : Exams and deadlines got me feeling overwhelmed. look at my to - do list! seems never - ending... Trying my best but been challenging. Deborah : Your efforts will bear fruit, don ' t give up! Jolene : Thanks, Deb. Any tips on studying or time management? Deborah : tip is to break it into smaller pieces set goals for yourself.For time management planners or schedulers help stay organized give time for yourself. Let me know if need help with study plan! Jolene : appreciate your help. Deborah : Take care good luck with exams. ' ll give you a mug like this one! encourages. Jolene : Thanks, Deb! cheered me up. All best with your classes. Bye! Deborah : Thanks, Jolene! Glad bring a smile to your face. Take care sure give yourself time to relax. Bye!

Deborah : Hi Jolene! We haven ' t corresponded for a long time! Jolene : Hey Deb, yeah life can get chaotic. How ' s it been going lately? Deborah : So much been going on lately. started yoga class in neighborhood - s good feeling! Now to share exercise with neighbors watch it transform them. Jolene : Congrats. How did you do this? Deborah : Thanks! neighbors interested in trying yoga hosted class for them on Friday. great to see everyone embrace enjoy it. Here is our photo together. Jolene : ' s cool! What made want to start teaching it? Deborah : find it calming wanted to share with others. Giving people peace and awareness brings me much happiness. Jolene : Wow, Deb! ' s awesome when can share something we love make things better for others. Deborah : Teaching it is awesome because can help others made great friends through it. nice for building community connections. Jolene : motivating. great to have support in tough times. Deborah : s one of life ' s best parts, right? Jolene : having someone to rely on is key in tough times. makes difference in how we handle life. Plus ' s something I wanted to tell you. Deborah : What ' s up? I ' m listening. We ' ll figure it out. Jolene : hard time dealing with Engineering assignments. a lot to manage struggling to keep up. Can still talk about time management? Deborah : Sure, Jolene. Let ' s find a time that works for both of us. Jolene : find time to chat - ll check my schedule get back to you. Deborah : Take your time, Jolene. ' ll work it out. Take care of yourself, OK? Jolene : ' ll make sure to take it. See you soon! Deborah : ' m here for you if you need me. ' s catch up soon. Jolene : Have a great day

Deborah : Hey Jolene, it ' s been a while. Hope you ' re doing okay with all your exams and deadlines. know ' s difficult for you right now. Jolene : Hey Deb! Yeah can be tough. Trying to find time for everything is like playing catch - up - stressful! Deborah : How manage your time stay organized with all projects and deadlines? Jolene : using Pomodoro Technique - 25 minutes work, 5 - minute break - to avoid burnout but still struggling to prioritize. any other tips on time management? Deborah : create daily schedule or to - do list. s my example for today. Jolene : tried making one but overwhelming when big stack of tasks. example from last Friday. Deborah : tried breaking it down or prioritizing tasks? Jolene : can often feel overwhelming difficult to figure out where to start. Deborah : get it, Jolene. When overloaded, use certain method. helps me figure out what ' s important and urgent more organized. Do you know about it? Jolene : Nah not familiar with that one. What ' s it about? Deborah : Want me to tell you about it? helps you organize things based on how important and urgent. Jolene : Sure tell me more about it! sounds useful. Deborah : Eisenhower Matrix sorts tasks into four boxes, categorizing them based on urgency importance. can be useful for organizing prioritizing. ' a breakdown. Jolene : visualization is helpful too. Thanks for sharing! Deborah : glad, was helpful. s give it a try see if helps stay focused less stressed. Jolene : Yeah, ' ll give it a go. Fingers crossed it ' ll help me. Thanks for the help! Deborah : Don ' t forget to take it easy look after yourself. Wishing you all the best! Recently, Anna and I were sitting by the sea, watching sunset talking about each other. realized we inspire each other.thoughts does sea in this photo make you think of? Jolene : gets me thinking of when I ' ll learn to surf. Gotta find spare time! Deborah : Surfing, huh Jolene? Chase your dreams, don ' t be daunted. thought about steps can take? Jolene : Definitely! gathering information watching videos got beginners ' guide to surfing need to find right time place to get lesson. Deborah : Way to go! Taking first steps is key. Believe in yourself keep going! Jolene : Thanks for support! got me pumped to try surfing. keep pushing myself to make it happen. Deborah : Keep it up, Jolene! Remember, experience matters as much as end result. Step by step have fun along way. You can do it! Jolene : Thanks for boost

Jolene : Hey Deb, long time no talk. A lot ' s happened! On Friday had breakthrough with my engineering project. Finally found solution to a prob s bugging me. Feels great to see my hard work paying off. Deborah : proven your skills. Feels great, right? Keep it up! bought new props for yoga class! Here it in action. Jolene : also have new details for this case! Rate it! Deborah : That s cool! bought this candle for atmosphere to improve yoga practice. How about you? When do it, what feelings do you get? Jolene : feel relaxed during this activity. love creating serene space with soothing scents like lavender and rosemary. any favorite scents or rituals for it? Deborah : also big fan of scents like this! Candles and essential oils add warmth and calm to yoga session s amazing certain smells can transport you to place of peace. any other ways to enhance yoga practice? Jolene : find music helps me. Any favorite tracks? Deborah : find instrumental tracks with mellow melodies and rhythms help create peaceful vibe. One of favorites is track called " Savana. " What songs / artists like listening to during your practice? Jolene : love listening to Nils Frahm and Olafur Arnalds during my practice. Their music calming puts me in different headspace. any recommendations for other similar artists or tracks should check out? Deborah : listening to album called ' Sleep, ' great for meditation and deep relaxation. Hope find it as calming as I do! Jolene : Thanks for the tips, Deborah! gonna check them out. always good to have new tunes for yoga! Deborah : Let me know your thoughts on albums! Jolene : See you! Deborah : Take care and keep up good work

Deborah : Hey Jolene! Great to see you! Had blast biking nearby with my neighbor last week - freeing beautiful. Checked out art show with friend today - cool inspiring stuff. Reminded me of my mom. Jolene : Hey Deborah! Sounds like you had blast biking and at art show. Your photo looks like you were into it! make think profound thoughts? Deborah : My mom interested in art. believed art could give out strong emotions connect us. When I go to art show, ' s like we ' re still experiencing it together even though she ' s gone. hard but comforting. Jolene : Losing someone is hard, but finding something helps you cope is great. Deborah : Finding ways to keep her memory alive gives me peace. amazing something simple like artwork can bring back powerful emotions remind us of those we ' ve lost. about finding solace in things we love, art has done that for me. Jolene : Even my snakes can ' t chat or understand what I going through our time together is valuable teaches me to take time be in tune with myself. playing video games with my partner after long day great way for to relax. Deborah : Simple things can bring us most happiness. How activities helped you during tough times? Jolene : brings us closer together! Deborah : comfort can be helpful when times get tough. Jolene : been working on big project lately - tough cool to watch it take shape. Can ' t wait to see final result! Deborah : waiting to hear how everything turns out. Keep up good work! Jolene : Thanks Deb! Your support means a lot. ll keep you updated on progress of project. Deborah : Glad my support means a lot to you! ' ll always be here for you. Jolene : Take care! Deborah : Enjoy your day make time for things that bring you joy. See ya! Jolene : ' ll make sure to find time for that.Have a good one

Jolene : Hey Deborah! Long time no talk - had lots of stuff going on. Remember tough engineering project? I finally wrapped that up last month. Look at result! Deborah : Jolene! Congrats on wrapping up your with it! You put in the work and it paid off. Jolene : proud of myself for sticking it out despite problems and finishing it. ' s big milestone. Deborah : Now you ' ve reached this big milestone, what do you have planned next? Jolene : I ' m interning at a well-known engineering firm. s great opportunity to test my skills gain real - world experience. These my new colleagues! Deborah : What ' s best part of it so far? Jolene : best part far to apply what I learned in school to real projects. s fulfilling to see my ideas come to life. Deborah : Mind if ask how internship impacted you? Jolene : had positive impact on my life. stoked my love of engineering encouraged me to keep striving for my dreams. Deborah : How it inspired you to keep striving for your dreams? Jolene : Hanging out with people who love what they do inspired me to stay focused keep working towards my goals. s shown me with dedication and effort, anything is possible. Deborah : to find good work - life balance during internship? Jolene : finding balance tough. been slogging away s hard to make time for my hobbies and chilling out. Deborah : considered taking breaks finding activities like yoga to help relax and unwind? might make a difference. Jolene : Yeah m trying to do it. s example of how spent yesterday morning, yoga on top of mount Talkeetna. Deborah : Nice job, Jolene! How long have you been doing yoga and meditation? looks like ' s helping you regroup and recharge. Jolene : ' ve been doing them sporadically for about 3 years now had real positive effect on me.Deborah : Has it benefited you in any way? found it helpful in difficult moments? Jolene : helped me with stress kept me centered. Deborah : Glad they ' ve been helpful for you! Jolene : No idea how I would ' ve survived without them! Deborah : my favorite studio always so calming. Jolene : wondering if any advice on calming mind breathing during yoga? Deborah : recommend practicing mindful breathing for yoga. Set aside few minutes each day to sit with eyes closed take deep breaths focus on how air feels entering leaving your body. Jolene : Thanks, Deborah! ll give it a try. Appreciate advice! Deborah : If need more tips or help let me know. Take care! Jolene : See you

Deborah : Hey Jolene! How ' s it going? haven ' t talked in a while.
